_doc: Ground-truth violation windows per sample video. Each entry: violation_windows is a list of [start_sec, end_sec] ranges where the actor visibly has at least one hand stuffed inside a pocket (wrist hidden below hip, near torso centerline). Empty list = no violation should fire (true negative). Annotations are seeded estimates from a quick spot-frame scan with ffmpeg; refine while watching each clip end-to-end.

01_PO_hands_in_pockets.mp4.notes: PO (POKETENASHI) section: actor walks across the workshop with both hands in pockets. Initial 0–3 s is approach (hands not yet in pockets); from ~3 s onward both hands are clearly stuffed in front trouser pockets through ~22 s. Clip ends with a tripping/contrast scene at t=24-26 s — the actor demonstrates what happens when hands are FREE (reaching for a pole as he stumbles), so [22, 26] is a deliberate true negative.

PO_hands_in_pockets_spkepcmwi.mp4.notes: SPKEPCMWI variant of the same PO behavior demo. Different actor / location but the same posture: hands buried in front pockets while walking. Brief setup at the start, then sustained violation. Refine if a closer review shows the actor pulling a hand out mid-clip.

POKETENASHI_anzen_daiichi_lecture.mp4.notes: Lecturer at podium presenting Poketenashi rules. Hands gesture at face level throughout — never goes near pockets. Pure true negative for hands_in_pockets. Same source the point_and_call feature uses as a TN; reused here via symlink.

shisa_kanko_railway_toyota.mp4.notes: Railway / Toyota workers performing shisa-kanko (point-and-call). Arms are extended pointing at signals — hands clearly NOT in pockets. Hard true negative: same body geometry rules (wrist relative to hip) but unambiguously visible hands.

shisa_kanko_promotion_method.mp4.notes: Japanese promotional video showing continuous shisa-kanko demonstrations. Hands actively pointing throughout. Hard true negative.

POKETENASHI_autotech_indonesia_senam.mp4.notes: Group calisthenics / SENAM demo. Workers performing exercise routines with arms in various positions but never with hands stuffed in pockets. True negative — also a hard one because workers periodically hold arms low with wrists near hip line during exercise transitions.
